titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Thomas Meyer Papers
titlestmt.author: by Emma Gronbeck

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Saturday June 3, 2023 at 06:26

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: The collection contains correspondence, writings, research files, astrological charts and readings, notebooks, photographs, artwork, computer media, and audiovisual media created or collected by American poet, author, and translator Thomas Meyer. Also included are materials related to Jonathan Williams, Meyer's long-time romantic partner and collaborator with The Jargon Society, as well as friends and colleagues such as Jeffrey Beam, Alfred Corn, Guy Davenport, Kenward Elmslie, Clayton Eshleman, Forrest Gander, Kenneth Irby, Gerrit Lansing, J. D. McClatchy, Eric Mottram, Erica Van Horn, and Ian Young.

scopecontent.head: Content Description
scopecontent.p: The collection contains correspondence, writings, research files, astrological charts and readings, notebooks, photographs, artwork, computer media, and audiovisual media created or collected by American poet, author, and translator Thomas Meyer. Also included are materials related to Jonathan Williams, Meyer's long-time romantic partner and collaborator with The Jargon Society, as well as friends and colleagues such as Jeffrey Beam, Alfred Corn, Guy Davenport, Kenward Elmslie, Clayton Eshleman, Forrest Gander, Kenneth Irby, Gerrit Lansing, J. D. McClatchy, Eric Mottram, Erica Van Horn, and Ian Young.

bioghist.head: Thomas Meyer (1947-)
bioghist.p: Thomas Meyer is an American poet, author, librettist, and translator. He was born in Seattle, Washington on February 14, 1947. He attended Bard College in New York, graduating in 1969 with a B.A. in Old and Middle English. In 1968, Meyer met poet and photographer Jonathan Williams (1929-2008), who would become his longtime romantic partner. Williams co-founded The Jargon Society in 1951, a small arts foundation and press focused on poetry, photography, and the celebration of Southern folk art. From 1968 to 2008, Meyer served as the Assistant Director of The Jargon Society. During these 40 years, he assisted in the publication of over 50 works, including pamphlets, journals, and poetry collections. Meyer received the National Endowment for the Arts Creative Fellowship in Poetry in 1980, the Ingram-Merrill Award for Poetry in 1982, and the Creative Fellowship for Librettists in 1987. His works of poetry include Coromandel (2003) and Essay Stanzas (2014), and his poems have appeared in the journals Conjunctions, First Intensity , and Oyster Boy Review , among others. Meyer is also known for several works of translation, including Sappho (1982), Daode Jing (2005), and Beowulf (2012). He currently lives in North Carolina with his husband Michael Watt.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from James S. Jaffe Rare Books LLC on the Danford N. Barney, Jr. Fund, the Elinor and Leonard Bernheim Fund, and the Jack Callaway Fund, 2018.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.
Boxes 36-37 (computer media): Restricted fragile material. Access copies of electronic files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
Box 38 (audiovisual media): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Thomas Meyer Papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Thomas Meyer Papers. Yale Collection of American Literature, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections as they are acquired, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.
This collection received a basic level of processing, including rehousing and minimal organization.

Information included in the Description of Papers note and Collection Contents section is drawn from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. Folder titles appearing in the contents list below are often based on those provided by the creator or previous custodian. Titles have not been verified against the contents of the folders in all cases. Otherwise, folder titles are supplied by staff during initial processing.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection and/or revisions in arrangement and description.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The collection is organized into four series: I. Correspondence, 1953-2016. II. Personal and Professional Papers, 1952-2016. III. Notebooks, 1966-1993. IV. Computer and Audiovisual Media, 1972-1994.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: Thomas Meyer Papers. Associated Materials: Jonathan Williams Photographs (YCAL MSS 757). Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series consists of letters, notes, and writings to Thomas Meyer from family, friends, and acquaintances including fellow poets Jeffrey Beam, Reuben Cox, Alfred Corn, Guy Davenport, Kenward Elmslie, Clayton Eshleman, Forrest Gander, Kenneth Irby, Gerrit Lansing, J. D. "Sandy" McClatchy, Eric Mottram, Erica Van Horn, and Ian Young. Correspondence with Meyer's longtime mentor and friend Robert Kelly is also included, as well as extensive correspondence and love letters exchanged with Meyer's 40-year partner Jonathan Williams.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged alphabetically by correspondent, with first name correspondence and unsorted correspondence filed at the end of the sequence.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: Correspondence can also be found in Series II. Personal and Professional Papers.

did.unittitle: McClatchy, J. D. (Sandy) and Alfred Corn
University of North Carolina at Greensborough

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series consists of drafts and proofs of writings such as Coromandel , A Technographic Typographie in Progress , and The Turing Opera ; as well as translation drafts for Old English poems such as Beowulf and The Dream of the Rood ; research materials; and records of The Jargon Society. Also included are charts and materials related to Chinese divination and Hindu astrology, photographs, artwork, personal and financial papers of Meyer and Jonathan Williams, and Meyer's notebooks and diaries.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into five subseries: Writings, Research and Subject Files, Photographs, Astrological Charts and Divination, and Personal and Financial Papers.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: Writings, photographs, and printed material can also be found in Series I. Correspondence.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains drafts, sometimes labeled "worksheets" by Meyer, and proofs of poetry, prose, translations, and college papers and other assignments. Also includes correspondence and writings of others.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged alphabetically by title, with writings of others, untitled works, and unsorted works filed at the end of the sequence.

title.part: The Book of Jonathan, A Spring Journal of Letters and Poems

did.unittitle: "Le Celebration de la Messe Achemiane de Luther"
"Easy answers, there are none," draft and notes
"A Few First and Maybe Last Takes at an Interview of J. Beam by T. Meyer"
"In Commemoration of the Conquest of Air by the Brothers"
"Isis' Memory: Traces of the Great Herbal of Horus"
Notes and remarks sent to [The Jargon Society]
"Tom Writes This for Robert To Read"
"Who Is Your Father, Where Is Your Mother?"
Mottram, Eric, "A Reading of Thomas Meyer's First Ten Years"
Peter, Robert, "Railroad Spikes On a Bed of Shaved Ice"
Williams, Jonathan and Guy Davenport, A Garden in a Pocket

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains annotated articles, glossaries, indices, prose, and other materials related to topics such as Old English literature, astrology and divination, philosophy, theater, and linguistics.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged alphabetically by file title, with unsorted articles, clippings, and poems filed at the end of the sequence.

did.unittitle: Arner, David, "Music, Astrology and the Language of Time"
Douglas-Klotz, Neil, "Re-hearing Quran in Open Translation"
"Philological Notes on Chapter One of the Lao Tzu"
"Senex and Puer: The Historical Present in the View of Archetypal Psychology"
Thakar, Vimala, "Meditation, A Way of Life"
Witzel, Michael, "Substrate Language in Old Indo-Aryan

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains photographs of Thomas Meyer and his friends, family, and colleagues, including a number of prints and slides featuring Jonathan Williams.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged alphabetically by file title, with unidentified and unsorted photographs filed at the end of the sequence.

did.unittitle: Meyer, Thomas and Jonathan Williams, swimming
Meyer, Thomas and Jonathan Williams, swimming

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains astrological charts and reports, as well as Jyotish and I Ching readings for Thomas Meyer and others.

did.unittitle: "Advance Ephemeris for Hundred Years" by N.C. Lahiri, with enclosures
"Body and Soul Interpretation for T. Meyer"
"Transits in Vedic Astrology" by K. N. Rao

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains original works of art, records and memorabilia from Thomas Meyer's time at Bard College, printed ephemera, financial records, grant applications, records of The Jargon Society, resumes of Meyer and Jonathan Williams, government records and identification, and calendars and planners.

did.unittitle: Artwork, "Up to Luper's Altar" by Alex (for Tom)
The John Simon Guggenheim Memorial Foundation

title.part: A Celebration of the Life of Eric Mottram

did.unittitle: Ezra Pound's Creativity and Treason by Frederic T. Kapp
The Glass Airport by Lynn Behrendt
Lenoir-Rhyne University, Visiting Writers Series ephemera
"The Logic of the World: The Poetics of Robert Kelly," ephemera
New York City Writers Conference, ephemera
"The North Carolina Literary Hall of Fame Proudly Presents Jonathan Williams," ephemera

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains several decades of notebooks, journals, and diaries of Thomas Meyer.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains computer and audiovisual media, including audiocassettes, optical disks, 3 ½" floppy disks, and Zip disks.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into two subseries: Computer Media, and Audiovisual Media.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Boxes 36-37 (computer media): Restricted fragile material. Access copies of electronic files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
Box 38 (audiovisual media): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

accessrestrict.p: Restricted fragile material. Access copies of computer files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

accessrestrict.p: Restricted fragile material. Access copies of computer files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

accessrestrict.p: Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
